name: Spotted Chocolate Macadamia Nut Torte
recipeIngredient: 3 1/2 oz Jar salted macadamia nuts, chopped (reserve 6)
1 tbsp White chocolate liqueur or creme de cacao
1 oz Milk chocolate or semisweet chocolate, melted, for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. Grease a 9 or 10 inch springform pan.
3. Combine brownie mix, water, oil, and eggs in a bowl; beat with a hand mixer until well combined (about 50 strokes).
4. Stir in white chocolate pieces and chopped macadamia nuts. Pour mixture into prepared pan.
5. Bake for 45 to 50 minutes or until the center of the cake is set. Allow cooling for one hour, then remove sides of the pan and let cool completely.
6. For topping: Melt white chocolate in a heavy saucepan over low heat or using a double boiler. Stir in cream and liqueur until smooth.
7. Spread melted white chocolate mixture evenly over the cooled torte and allow it to drip down the sides.
8. Melt milk chocolate or semisweet chocolate in a heavy saucepan over low heat.
9. Drop spoonfuls of the melted chocolate onto the top of the cake to create a polka-dotted effect. Place reserved whole macadamia nuts in an attractive pattern on top of the cake.
10. Refrigerate for one hour before serving. Makes 16 servings.
